6f11dfc7-c45b-424a-a212-5c3cca34f538.title: Classification of Coupling in Software Engineering

bullets.text: Classification of Coupling in Software Engineering
Data, Stamp, Control, Common, and Content Coupling

learning_objectives: Understand the concept of coupling in software engineering
Identify and classify different types of coupling (Data, Stamp, Control, Common, and Content)
Analyze the impact of coupling on software modularity and design

90af4509-32b1-4037-b14c-daed0d2dea64.title: What is Coupling?

bullets.text: • Coupling indicates the degree of interdependence between two modules.
• High coupling means modules are heavily interdependent.
• High coupling → harder to understand, maintain, and develop independently.

learning_objectives: Understand the concept of coupling in software design
Identify high and low coupling in modules
Recognize the factors that determine coupling, such as interface complexity and parameter passing

143c16d7-8290-432d-882d-a77050ce22f6.title: Data Coupling

bullets.text: • Two modules communicate by passing elementary data items (int, float, char).
• Data must be problem-related, not control-related.
• Example: A function passing an integer value as a parameter.

learning_objectives: Understand the concept of data coupling and its importance in software design.
Identify and distinguish between problem-related and control-related data.
Apply data coupling principles to improve modularity and reduce coupling in software systems.

6f50ffe6-514b-46c1-a7c5-cefff1adbb01.title: Stamp Coupling

bullets.text: • Two modules communicate using composite data items.

learning_objectives: Understand the concept of Stamp Coupling and its characteristics
Identify the differences between Stamp Coupling and Data Coupling
Explain how composite data items are used in Stamp Coupling
Design and implement modular software using Stamp Coupling principles

35d627d9-761c-4fbe-b5b2-9f89f40705cd.title: Control Coupling

bullets.text: • One module controls the execution of another via passed data.
• Example: A flag set in one module and tested in another.

learning_objectives: Understand the concept of control coupling and its implications on software design
Identify examples of control coupling in software modules
Explain how control coupling leads to tighter interdependence between modules
Apply principles of modularity to reduce control coupling and improve software maintainability

6c4ebb1e-8f73-47ea-8dbb-0bc369605726.title: Common Coupling

bullets.text: • Two or more modules share global data.
• High risk of unintended side effects.

learning_objectives: Identify common coupling in software design
Explain the risks associated with common coupling
Describe the impact of common coupling on debugging and maintenance

23de3f8a-6860-4fe6-8c94-f1804a3db512.title: Content Coupling

bullets.text: • One module directly uses or modifies code of another.
• Example: Jumps into another module’s code.
• Not supported in modern high-level languages (e.g., C).
• Highest degree of coupling, most undesirable.

learning_objectives: Understand the concept of content coupling and its implications on software design.
Identify and analyze examples of content coupling in code.
Explain the reasons why content coupling is considered the highest degree of coupling and most undesirable.

5e70b432-e479-4cc9-bd9d-1f345e3a59ec.title: Coupling Severity Order

bullets.text: Higher coupling → Higher complexity and maintenance cost.

learning_objectives: Understand the concept of coupling severity order in software engineering.
Identify and explain the differences between data coupling, stamp coupling, and control coupling.
Apply the coupling severity order to assess and improve the modularity of software systems.

a8728f36-17de-49de-95d9-8b88714ee90f.title: Classification of Coupling in Software Engineering
